pages.title: Welcome to PSYCH-101
pages.html:
Welcome!

Hello and welcome to Introduction to Psychology. I am Dr. Rivera and I am excited to guide you through this journey. Please read the syllabus carefully and reach out if you have any questions via Canvas Inbox. Office hours are Tuesdays 2–4 PM via Zoom.
pages.text: Welcome! Hello and welcome to Introduction to Psychology. I am Dr. Rivera and I am excited to guide you through this journey. Please read the syllabus carefully and reach out if you have any questions via Canvas Inbox. Office hours are Tuesdays 2–4 PM via Zoom.

pages.title: Module 1 Overview
pages.html:
Module 1: Introduction to Psychology

By the end of this module you will be able to: (1) define psychology and describe its major subfields, (2) explain the scientific method as applied to psychological research, (3) identify key historical figures in the development of psychology.

Watch the lecture video below and take notes using the Cornell Note template provided.

pages.title: Week 1 Reading
pages.html:
Week 1 Reading

Read Chapter 1 of your textbook. As you read, pay attention to the definitions highlighted in yellow and consider how each perspective would explain a common behavior like procrastination.

After reading, complete the Reading Reflection form.
pages.text: Week 1 Reading. Read Chapter 1 of your textbook. As you read, pay attention to the definitions highlighted in yellow and consider how each perspective would explain a common behavior like procrastination. After reading, complete the Reading Reflection form.

assignments.title: Week 1 Reflection Essay
assignments.html:
Week 1 Reflection Essay

Write a 300–500 word reflection on how you have experienced psychology in your daily life. Submit as a PDF or Word document by Sunday at 11:59 PM.

Grading: This assignment is worth 50 points. You will be evaluated using the attached rubric covering content (30 pts), clarity (10 pts), and formatting (10 pts).

Feedback will be provided within 5 business days via Canvas SpeedGrader comments.

Feedback will be provided within 5 business days via Canvas SpeedGrader comments.

discussions.title: Discussion: Who Studies Psychology?
discussions.html:
Discussion Prompt

Introduce yourself and share one reason you are interested in psychology. Then respond to at least two classmates with a substantive comment connecting their interest to a psychological concept you have encountered.

Guidelines: Be respectful and curious. Posts are due Wednesday; responses by Sunday.
discussions.text: Discussion Prompt. Introduce yourself and share one reason you are interested in psychology. Then respond to at least two classmates with a substantive comment connecting their interest to a psychological concept you have encountered. Guidelines: Be respectful and curious. Posts are due Wednesday; responses by Sunday.

syllabus.html:
PSYCH-101 Syllabus

Welcome to Introduction to Psychology. This course surveys the major areas of psychology including biological bases, learning, cognition, development, personality, and social behavior.

Course Policies

Academic Honesty: All submitted work must be your own. AI use: You may use AI tools for brainstorming but all final writing must be your own words. Any AI use must be disclosed in your submission. Plagiarism will result in a zero and may be reported to the Dean of Students.

Technology

This course uses Canvas, Zoom, and Google Forms. All tools are accessible and meet institutional requirements. If you encounter technical difficulties, contact the Help Desk at helpdesk@college.edu or call (555) 123-4567.

Student Support

Disability Services: Contact the DSP office at dsp@college.edu. Tutoring: Free tutoring available at the Learning Center (Building A, Room 101). Library: library.college.edu. Counseling: counseling@college.edu. Basic Needs: basicneeds@college.edu.

Communication

I will respond to Canvas Inbox messages within 48 hours (Monday–Friday). I initiate weekly announcements every Monday morning to guide your week.

Feedback

At the end of the course, please complete the anonymous Course Evaluation survey to help me improve this class.
